quote_text: California is now the first state in the nation to offer full-scope Medi-Cal to all income-eligible adults regardless of immigration status. This is about values. This is about what it means to be a Californian.
We won't cooperate with any state that tries to prosecute women or doctors for receiving or providing reproductive care. California will be a sanctuary — full stop.
With Prop 1, California has made a powerful statement: reproductive freedom is a fundamental right. We will not go backwards.
Congress must reclaim its constitutional authority over trade. The Founders gave that power to the people's representatives — not to one person in the White House to impose tariffs on a whim.

We signed a budget that closes a $46.8 billion deficit while protecting the things that matter most to Californians — education, healthcare, housing. We did it without raising taxes on working families.
This door's wide open now. It's going to happen, whether you like it or not.

Today we celebrate the progress we've made — not just in California, but across the country — to ensure that every American has the freedom to marry the person they love. We will never stop fighting to protect that freedom.
Conversion therapy is a fraud. It's a lie. It causes immeasurable harm to the people subjected to it, particularly young people who are already vulnerable. California will not tolerate it.

I have long supported the rights of transgender kids, and that is not going to change. But I also want to make sure that we are thoughtful about implementation and that we are working collaboratively with schools.
I met with President Zelenskyy today in Sacramento. California stands with the people of Ukraine. We believe in democracy. We believe in sovereignty. We believe in the rule of law.
I'm directing the state to phase out the sale of all new gas-powered passenger cars by 2035. This is the most impactful step our state can take to fight climate change.
California is now a permanent vote-by-mail state. Every registered voter will receive a ballot at home. Voting should be easy, accessible, and secure — and California is leading the way.

California will not be complicit in policies that tear families apart and sow terror in our communities. We are a state that defends the dignity and rights of all people, regardless of immigration status.
California has always said no to mass deportations. We protect our values. We protect our people.
I share the concerns of those who believe AI poses risks. But SB 1047 doesn't actually focus on the specific harms. It focuses on the size of the model, not the conduct. That's why I vetoed it.
We are signing 17 bills today that address real and specific harms from AI: deepfakes in elections, AI in healthcare decisions, worker protections. California is leading on AI accountability.
This is the most significant package of climate legislation in the history of the United States. California is setting the tone for the rest of the world.

Creating this task force is a powerful signal that California is facing its history, honestly and directly. The descendants of enslaved people deserve a process that examines the harm done and charts a path forward.
We're in a housing crisis. For too long, we have allowed too few homes to be built, and too many Californians to be left behind. These new laws will cut the red tape and build more housing faster.
The housing crisis isn't just an economic issue — it's a moral crisis. We cannot allow California to be a place where only the wealthy can afford to live. These new housing laws change that.
Voters have a right to know who is funding campaigns. Dark money has corrupted our political process. Today we're making California's disclosure laws the strongest in the nation.
California will continue to be a welcoming state. We will continue to protect the dignity and rights of immigrants who call California home, regardless of what Washington decides to do.
Deepfake election ads are a direct threat to democracy. Voters deserve to know when they're being manipulated by AI-generated content. California is going to lead on this.
We are expanding Medi-Cal so that no Californian is left behind — regardless of their immigration status. Healthcare coverage is not a privilege. It is a right.
Social Security is a promise we made to the American people. We should be expanding it, not cutting it — increasing benefits and making it solvent for the next generation.
Californians voted overwhelmingly to keep the independent redistricting commission. Politicians should not draw their own district lines. That's a conflict of interest that undermines democracy.

The Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade is an unconscionable attack on women's freedom and bodily autonomy. As Lieutenant Governor of California, I will do everything in my power to protect reproductive rights in our state.
Expanding Medi-Cal to all income-eligible adults regardless of immigration status is a moral imperative. Healthcare is a human right — not a privilege reserved for those with the right paperwork.

California is demonstrating to the world that we can be bold, ambitious, and science-driven. At COP26, I saw firsthand that states like California are filling the leadership vacuum left by national governments that continue to delay action.

At COP27, California continued to show the world what real climate leadership looks like. While others debate, we are acting — with policies, investments, and partnerships that are driving a clean energy future.
The fossil fuel era is ending. California is leading the transition to clean energy, and the rest of the world is watching. We don't need to choose between a strong economy and a healthy planet.

Our border communities are resilient, diverse, and deeply connected to Mexico — economically, culturally, and historically. Immigration policy must reflect that reality, not treat border communities as a threat to be managed.
On the anniversary of Obergefell, we celebrate love, equality, and the dignity of every person. California will always be a state where every family is valued and every person can live authentically.

Every Californian deserves an equal voice in our democracy. Permanent vote-by-mail means more voices are heard, more people participate, and our government is more representative of who we actually are.

The reparations task force is a critical step toward acknowledging the truth of our history and beginning to address the lasting harms of slavery and systemic racism. California is showing the nation that reconciliation is possible.
The housing crisis is a human crisis. California must build more homes — and build them where people want to live, near jobs and transit. Every community needs to be part of the solution.
Independent redistricting means voters choose their representatives — not the other way around. California's commission is a national model for how to take politics out of the mapmaking process.

I voted yes on the USMCA. It is a better deal for Indiana farmers and workers. We need to update these trade agreements to reflect the modern economy and protect American jobs.

Protecting fairness in women's sports is not complicated — it is a matter of basic fairness to female athletes who have trained and competed their entire lives. Indiana will stand for integrity in women's athletics.

Indiana will be a partner in enforcing federal immigration law. We believe in the rule of law, and that includes our immigration laws. We will cooperate fully with federal efforts to secure the border and enforce our laws.

The federal government has a responsibility to enforce our immigration laws, and Indiana will stand with those efforts. Communities are safer when the law is upheld. We fully support the enforcement actions being taken.

Indiana Republicans drew maps that reflect this state's conservative values and the will of Hoosier voters. The process was transparent, legal, and resulted in fair representation for Indiana communities.
The Supreme Court's ruling is a win for merit-based opportunity. Every American should be judged on what they've accomplished and what they bring to the table — not on their race or background.

Indiana's Religious Freedom Restoration Act is about protecting people of faith from government overreach. No one should be forced by the government to act against their sincerely held religious beliefs.
We need to focus on making healthcare more affordable for Hoosiers, not simply expanding government programs that are already unsustainable. Medicaid expansion without reforms is not the answer.
Hoosiers deserve to keep more of their hard-earned money. Cutting taxes — at the state and federal level — means more investment, more jobs, and more opportunity for Indiana families and businesses.

I am unashamedly pro-life. Life begins at conception, and it is the government's job to protect innocent human life. Indiana has been a leader in protecting the unborn, and I will continue that work as Lieutenant Governor.

We are living in a time when people of faith are being told they have to choose between their beliefs and participating in public life. That is wrong. Religious liberty is the first freedom, and I will fight for it every single day.
I believe marriage is between a man and a woman. That is what I have always believed, it is what my faith teaches, and it is what I will stand for in public office.

As the son of immigrants, I know what is at stake when we talk about immigration policy. My parents came to this country, worked hard, and built a life for our family. That is the American story. And the Dream Act would give millions of young people who grew up here the same chance my family had.

Every eligible American should be able to vote. We should make it easier, not harder. The John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act is about restoring and strengthening protections that have been gutted by the Supreme Court — protections that exist to ensure that all voices are heard.

Healthcare is a fundamental right. Every person in this country should have access to quality, affordable care — regardless of their income, their zip code, or their immigration status. The Medicare for All Act would make that a reality.

Women deserve to make their own reproductive health decisions. The Women's Health Protection Act is essential because states across the country have been racing to ban abortion and strip women of their constitutional rights. We must act to protect that freedom at the federal level.

California has experienced the devastating effects of climate change firsthand — wildfires, drought, extreme heat. We must act boldly and quickly to cut carbon emissions, invest in clean energy, and protect our communities from the worst impacts of the climate crisis.

These mass deportation raids are un-American. They are tearing families apart, instilling fear in immigrant communities, and undermining the values that define us as a nation. Due process is not optional — it is a constitutional guarantee.

Russia's invasion of Ukraine is a war of aggression against a sovereign democracy. If we allow Putin to succeed, we send a message to every authoritarian in the world that conquest pays. That is a lesson we cannot afford to teach. We must stand with Ukraine.

We are a nation of immigrants. The Dreamers are as American as any of us — they grew up here, went to school here, serve in our military. They deserve a permanent path to citizenship. Passing the Dream Act is a matter of basic fairness and fundamental decency.

A woman's right to make her own reproductive decisions is fundamental and personal. The government has no business making that decision for her. The overturning of Roe v. Wade was a devastating blow to women's rights and we must fight to restore those protections at the federal level.

George Floyd's death shocked the conscience of the nation. But his death cannot be in vain. The George Floyd Justice in Policing Act is a comprehensive response to the systemic racism in our criminal justice system — one that establishes real accountability and begins to restore trust.

Healthcare is a right, not a privilege. Every American deserves access to quality, affordable care. Medicare for All would guarantee that right and eliminate the fear that a serious illness will mean financial ruin. That is a promise worth keeping.

The right to vote is the foundation of our democracy. When states pass laws designed to make it harder for certain people to vote, that is not reform — that is suppression. The For the People Act would set national standards to protect voting rights and end partisan gerrymandering.

The time for waiting and talking and planning is over. Today, I am declaring a state of emergency on homelessness. This means we will move faster and do more to get people off the streets and into shelter, services and housing.
source_name: Los Angeles Mayor's Office Press Release

quote_text: George Floyd's murder laid bare what Black Americans have known for generations: that our criminal justice system is not delivering equal justice under the law. As chair of the Congressional Black Caucus, I am committed to transforming that system — to ensuring that Black lives truly matter in America.

Los Angeles is a city of immigrants, and we will not allow our immigrant communities to be terrorized. We are not going to cooperate with federal immigration enforcement operations that separate families and undermine public safety in our city.

Women have a fundamental right to make their own reproductive health decisions. The Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade is a devastating attack on that right, and Congress must act to restore the protections of Roe in federal law.

No one in this country should go bankrupt because they got sick. Healthcare is a right, and I am proud to support Medicare for All. We need a system that puts patients before profits and ensures every American has access to the care they need.

Mass deportations do not make our communities safer. They destroy families, create fear, and undermine the trust between immigrant communities and local law enforcement. Los Angeles will not be a partner in this cruelty.

The right to vote is the cornerstone of our democracy. We cannot allow partisan efforts to suppress the vote to stand unchallenged. The For the People Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act are essential to protecting every American's right to participate in our democracy.

The Respect for Marriage Act is consistent with my view that government shouldn't be in the business of telling people who they can love and who they can marry. I believe in limited government and individual liberty, and that applies here.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Respect for Marriage Act (S. 4556)

quote_text: The CHIPS and Science Act is about making sure America leads the world in the industries of the future — not just semiconductors, but artificial intelligence, quantum computing, and advanced manufacturing. We have to out-compete China, and this is how we do it.
source_name: U.S. Congress — CHIPS and Science Act (S. 4749)

quote_text: Trade agreements like the USMCA are the right way to open markets and protect American workers — not unilateral tariffs that invite retaliation and hurt Indiana's farmers, manufacturers, and exporters.

America must stand with our allies against aggression. The bipartisan national security supplemental we passed sends a clear message to Vladimir Putin: we will not abandon democracy in Ukraine, we will not allow him to redraw the map of Europe by force.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Emergency National Security Supplemental (S. 4109)

quote_text: I am pro-life. I have consistently voted to protect the unborn and to ensure taxpayer dollars are not used to fund abortions. These are core commitments I brought to the Senate and will continue to uphold.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Women's Health Protection Act (S. 4132)

quote_text: Protecting women's sports means ensuring that girls and women compete on a level playing field. The Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act reflects a principle that I think most Americans share — biological sex matters in athletic competition.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (S. 2617)

quote_text: I support border security and an orderly legal immigration system. Indiana's economy depends on legal pathways for workers and on enforcing the laws we have. We need comprehensive reform that secures the border first.

I voted no on the Ukraine supplemental because American taxpayers should not be writing blank checks to foreign governments when we have unsecured borders at home and record debt. America First means we take care of America first.

The No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion Act is simple: Americans who oppose abortion on moral and religious grounds should not be compelled to pay for it. Life begins at conception, and I will defend the unborn in every piece of legislation that comes before me.
source_name: U.S. Congress — No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion Act (H.R. 18)

quote_text: I introduced the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act because this is not a complicated issue. Women's sports exist to give women an equal competitive opportunity. Allowing biological males to compete destroys that. I will keep fighting until this is federal law.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (H.R. 426)

quote_text: The POLICE Act is a real alternative to the radical defund movement. It funds law enforcement, keeps communities safe, and holds bad actors accountable without painting every officer as a racist. We can have both accountability and respect for the men and women in blue.

Marriage is defined in the Bible and across human civilization as the union of a man and a woman. That is my sincere religious conviction and I will always vote to defend it. The Respect for Marriage Act is an attack on traditional marriage and I voted against it.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Respect for Marriage Act (S. 4556)

quote_text: The Tax Cuts and Jobs Act delivered real relief to working families and small businesses across Indiana. We need to make those cuts permanent. Every dollar that stays in the hands of Hoosiers is a dollar that creates jobs and builds our economy.
source_name: U.S. Congress — Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (H.R. 1)

quote_text: The crisis at our southern border is not an accident — it is the result of deliberate policy choices by the Biden administration to ignore the law. We need to finish the wall, reinstate Remain in Mexico, and enforce the laws on the books. That is the America First approach to immigration.
